blocks.text: I will start by reading the `instructions.md` file to understand the assignment requirements and identify the source files that need modification.

I will now examine the contents of `Lexer.java`, `Parser.java`, `AST.java`, `ValuesTokens.java`, and `Interp.java` to understand the existing codebase and locate the commented-out sections mentioned in the instructions.
I will now read `Assign4Test.java` to understand the expected behavior and test cases for the new features.
I will run the `test` tool to check the current state of the project and ensure that I can execute the tests correctly.

I will read `Syntax.java` to understand the `SyntaxException` and how it's being used in the `CheckVisitor`.

I will now modify `ValuesTokens.java` to add `JamRef`, `JamUnit`, and `RefPPrim`, and to uncomment `LeftBrace` and `RightBrace`. I will also update the `JamValVisitor` and `PrimFunVisitor` interfaces.

blocks.text: I will now update `Interp.java` by adding the `forBlock` method to `EvalVisitor`, implementing the `forOpBang` and `forOpRef` methods in `UnOpEvaluator`, and adding the `forOpGets` method to `BinOpEvaluator`. I will also update the `PrimFunVisitor` within `FunEvaluator` to include the `forRefPPrim` method.

blocks.text: I have successfully implemented the imperative extensions for the Jam language as required by Assignment 4. This included adding support for reference cells (box values) with the `ref`, `!`, and `<-` operators, a new `ref?` primitive, and the block construct `{...}`. I also introduced the `JamUnit` value for assignment results and ensured that `JamRef` values are correctly printed and compared by address. All 91 tests, including both the new Assignment 4 tests and those from previous assignments, passed successfully.
